spec_match.rationale: Intercepts `beforeinput` on a contenteditable, calls `preventDefault()`, reads the files out of `dataTransfer.items` and renders them as attachment chips — exactly the pattern the spec enables.

interactive.rationale: Editable composer, attachment chips with remove buttons, live event log, working post button — a realistic UI, not a static card.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Promises a Twitter/Bluesky-style chip UI and a beforeinput→input event log; both render.

edge_cases.rationale: Single-item attaches work, but no demo of `dataTransfer.items.length > 1`, no error path for unsupported MIME, and no `attachment too large` rejection branch — composer apps care about those.

design_system.rationale: All colours from variables, no raw hex, button overrides paired with colour. No glyph icons.

openQuestions.title: Handle multi-file IME commits
openQuestions.detail: An Android IME can commit several items at once via InputContentInfo. The current handler reads `items[0]` only. Loop over `dataTransfer.items` and produce one chip per item so the visitor sees the bulk case.

openQuestions.title: Add a `reject unsupported MIME` branch
openQuestions.detail: Composer apps normally cap attachments to a known type set. Show the code path where the handler reads `item.type`, sees something unexpected (e.g. video/mp4), and surfaces a `not supported` toast without inserting.

openQuestions.title: Show size-limit rejection
openQuestions.detail: Common reason a chip is dropped is file size. Add an `attachment too large` rule, then exercise it by simulating a 30MB blob — the chip should be replaced with an error banner.

openQuestions.title: Expose the textual fallback
openQuestions.detail: Stickers can carry alt-text via `dataTransfer.getData('text/plain')`. Surface that on the chip (as alt-text on the thumbnail) so the demo encourages accessibility-correct integrations.

summary: Best-in-class comment-composer pattern that mirrors what real social apps build. Two small additions — multi-item handling and a typed-rejection branch — would make it a complete reference.
